shortDef.en: An image stored as a fixed grid of pixels, each with a defined colour value.

definition.en: A raster image (also called a bitmap image) stores visual information as a rectangular grid of individual pixels. Each pixel has a specific colour value — typically defined by red, green, and blue channel values (RGB). The quality and detail of a raster image are directly tied to its resolution: the number of pixels wide and tall. When a raster image is scaled beyond its native resolution, the pixels become visible as a blocky, blurry effect (called pixelation). Common raster formats include PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, TIFF, and BMP. Raster images are the natural output of cameras, scanners, and screen captures — any device that captures light produces a raster image.

examples.en: A 1920×1080 JPG photograph contains 2,073,600 pixels, each with an RGB colour value.
A PNG screenshot of a web page is a raster image — scaling it up in an image editor reveals the individual pixels.
Digital camera RAW files are raster images at the sensor's native resolution.
